operating_state: Rental revenues increased to $3.3 billion driven primarily by the Duke Transaction acquisition adding 144 million square feet. Same-store NOI growth reached 8.9% with rent change on rollover of 73.4% reflecting strong market fundamentals. Occupancy remained elevated at 97.2% across the 1.2 billion square foot portfolio with consistent demand driving market rent increases. NAREIT FFO reached $3.0 billion for the six months ended June 30, 2023, compared to $2.1 billion in the prior year period.
cost_structure: Real Estate Segment NOI expanded with rental expenses increasing proportionally to revenue growth from acquired properties. Strategic Capital Segment NOI surged driven by promote revenues from USLF. General and administrative expenses increased due to higher equity-based compensation and inflationary pressures. Interest payments net of capitalized amounts increased significantly reflecting higher debt levels and rising rates.
material_events: Acquired 70-property U.S. portfolio for $3.1 billion in June funded through senior note issuances and credit facility draws. Issued $5.4 billion of senior notes with 4.7% weighted average rate and 14-year maturity during the first half. Established new $3.0 billion global credit facility in April with maturity in June 2027. Formed Prologis Japan Core Logistics Fund with initial contribution and earned promote revenues primarily from USLF third-party investors.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency exposure remains through investments in 19 countries with natural hedging via local currency borrowings and derivative instruments. Interest rate risk increased with new debt issuances at higher rates compared to historical portfolio. Supply chain disruption risks persist affecting customer demand patterns and development material costs.
forward_outlook: Development pipeline includes significant investment required through April 2025. Land bank supports potential future development providing long-term growth capacity. Lease mark-to-market of 66% provides organic growth runway even without additional market rent increases. Strategic capital business expansion planned through existing and new co-investment ventures with continued distributions expected from unconsolidated entities.
uncertainty_profile: Remaining equity commitments across co-investment ventures with various expiration dates through 2033. Redemption requests received in open-ended funds creating potential liquidity pressures. Development completion and leasing execution risks on pipeline with foreign currency translation impacts on international investments. Unconsolidated ventures carry third-party debt with no guarantees though performance impacts cash distributions received.
